title: indigofera sarmentosa
intro: Indigofera sarmentosa, also known as Bajari or Indian wisteria, is a perennial climber belonging to the pea family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits associated with Indigofera sarmentosa include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
